card.scanningSub: AI indexing and folder watch settings
card.archiveOpsSub: Path remapping, merge and extract operations
card.backupSub: Auto backup, snapshots and portable archive
card.performanceSub: Scan checkpoint and worker count settings
card.lanSub: Share or download archives over local network
card.devModeSub: Set up as a developer device

autoRagIndex.description: After scanning, new/changed files are processed in the background to be queryable by AI chat. This is CPU-intensive; if disabled, indexing must be started manually from the RAG panel.

folderWatch.enabledDescription: Detects added/removed/modified files inside scanned root folders and shows a toast notification in the bottom-right corner. When disabled, changes go unnoticed until you scan manually.
folderWatch.autoRescanToggle: Auto re-scan when changes are detected
folderWatch.autoRescanDescription: If no further change arrives for 60 seconds, that folder is re-scanned automatically. Skipped if a scan is already running. Default: off.

healthCheck.description: Check whether file records in the archive still exist on disk. You can bulk-clean orphaned records whose files are missing.
healthCheck.noOrphans: All files are in place — no issues found.
healthCheck.found: {{count}} orphaned record(s) found (file missing on disk).
healthCheck.error: An error occurred during the health check.

merge.description: Merge assets and related data from two archives into a single target archive.

extract.description: Copy or move assets matching filter criteria from an archive to a new or existing archive.

developerMode.description: Set this device as the developer machine to receive user feedback directly over LAN. The LAN server must be running to receive messages.
developerMode.serverWarning: LAN server could not be started — messages may not be received.

storage.locationUpdated: Database location updated. This location will be used on next restart.
storage.locationUpdateError: An error occurred while updating the database location.
storage.remapOldRequired: You must enter the old root path.

remap.description: If you have moved the project folder to a different drive, you can bulk-update all file paths.

backup.description: You can back up your archive in different ways.
backup.autoDescription: Creates hourly or manual backups on this device. You can revert to an earlier state after accidental deletes or changes. Up to 5 backups are kept; the oldest is auto-removed.
backup.portableDescription: Export your entire archive to a single .archivistpro file or restore from a backup. Use it to move to another computer or to store on a USB drive.

snapshot.maxDescription: Oldest snapshots beyond this limit are pruned automatically.
snapshot.confirmRestore: This will overwrite the current database with the selected backup.
snapshot.confirmDelete: This backup will be permanently deleted.

scan.checkpointDescription: How often to save the database to disk during scanning. Lower = safer (less data loss risk), higher = faster scanning.
scan.prepareWorkersDescription: How many files to extract hash + metadata + thumbnail for in parallel during scanning. HDD: 1-2 (seek thrash risk), SSD: 3-4, NVMe + 8 threads: 6-8, NVMe + 16+ threads (Ryzen 9, Threadripper, Xeon): 10-16. Higher values do not help on systems with few CPU cores.

trash.description: Deleted files are moved to the .archivistpro-trash/ folder. They can be restored with Undo.
trash.descriptionPart1: Deleted files are moved to the
trash.descriptionPart2: folder. They can be restored with Undo.

lan.description: You can download an archive from another device over LAN or share your archive on the network.

import.title: Restore Archive from Backup
import.remapHint: If the backup comes from another computer, replace the source root path with this machine's path.
import.remapHintMulti: {{count}} source folders detected in the backup. Pick the new path on this machine for each.
import.skipHint: Rows with empty new path are skipped.

oda.description: ODA FileConverter is used to extract layer/block/text information from DWG files with full accuracy. If not installed, raw binary scanning will be used as fallback.
oda.bundledNotAvailable: Bundled installer not available, trying online methods...
oda.installedButNotDetected: Installation complete but ODA path could not be detected automatically. Please click 'Auto Detect' or enter the path manually.
oda.downloadPageOpened: Download page opened. Click 'Auto Detect' after installation.
oda.installFailed: Auto install failed, download page opened
oda.noInstallerFound: Auto install failed. Please select the ODA installer from your computer.

security.auditRetentionDesc: Audit log entries older than this are cleaned up at app startup. Choose ∞ to keep everything.
security.loginMaxAttemptsDesc: Users exceeding this count are locked out. Window equals the lockout duration.
security.loginLockoutDesc: When max attempts is exceeded, the account stays locked for this long.

about.description: All AI processing runs on your local machine. Your files are never sent to any server.
about.extractorsDesc: Metadata fields extracted from each file format. Click a row to see details.

welcome.title: Welcome to Archivist Pro!
welcome.desc: Let us show you around. This quick tour will introduce the key features. You can skip anytime.

scan.title: Scan Your Files
scan.desc: Add your project folders and start scanning. DWG, MAX, SKP, PDF and 80+ file formats are automatically recognized and archived.

search.title: Smart Search
search.desc: Find your files using keyword or semantic search. With AI indexing enabled, it also catches similar concepts.

viewModes.title: View Modes
viewModes.desc: Use Explorer view for file cards, Dashboard for statistics, and Technical view for detailed tables.

aiChat.title: AI Assistant
aiChat.desc: Ask natural language questions about the files in your archive. AI analyzes file contents to provide answers.

settings.title: Settings
settings.desc: Configure user management, security, AI settings, backups, and language preferences here.

done.title: You're All Set!
done.desc: You've learned the basics. You can restart this tour anytime from Settings. Happy archiving!

welcome.title: Welcome!
welcome.subtitle: Let's check your system before you start using Archivist Pro.
welcome.wasmFail: WASM is not supported — app may not work properly

index.autoPreparing: Preparing metadata for AI Chat in background

index.badgeTooltip: Meta: metadata indexed · Content: file body indexed

input.placeholder: Ask your archive — '/visual <text>' for image search (Enter = send)

sessions.title: Chats
sessions.empty: No chats yet. Start with "+ New".

prepareIndex.label: Prepare...
prepareIndex.tooltip: Index files that aren't yet ready for AI chat

diagnostics.dimMismatch: {{skipped}} chunks skipped — embedding dimension mismatch (query {{queryDim}}, DB has {{dbDims}}). Re-index the files.

chat.noGpu: No GPU detected on this machine. AI chat is only available on GPU-enabled machines. You can use the search feature in the left panel.

fallback.reranker: Reranker timed out — default ranking was used.
fallback.queryRewrite: Query enrichment failed — original query was used.

help.title: AI Chat — Feature Guide
help.chunkDesc: We split a file's text into small pieces (~500 words) that AI can process. A mathematical signature (embedding) is generated for each chunk, and search works on these.
help.embeddingDesc: A 384-dimensional number list representing a text's meaning. Embeddings of similar texts are close to each other.
help.metaChunkDesc: A special chunk generated from a file's metadata only (file name, project, tags, DWG layers). Enables searching DWG/MAX files that have no body text.
help.citationDesc: Sources marked with "[1]", "[2]" etc. in the answer. Click to navigate to the source file.
help.hallucinationDesc: When AI fabricates information not present in sources. The system prevents this with keyword checks + strict prompt rules.
help.llmDesc: Large Language Model — the AI model that generates answers. Runs locally on Ollama. Recommended: qwen3:8b · Minimum: qwen3:4b
help.flowDesc: Write a question → system searches indexed files → feeds the most relevant chunks to the LLM → generates a sourced answer. Response streams word by word.
help.slashVisualDesc: Searches for images instead of text. E.g.: /visual staircase plan → CLIP semantic visual search. Short form: /g
help.badgeRerank: When on: top-20 chunks are retrieved, LLM picks the best 6 by relevance. +1 LLM call, more accurate answer. When off: direct top-8 embedding results.
help.badgeEnrich: When on, short queries are expanded with synonyms + English equivalents via LLM. Queries longer than 5 words are considered specific and left unchanged.
help.badgeScope: Narrow search scope: select a specific project or tag → search only those files.
help.indexBtnDesc: Splits missing files' body text into chunks and generates embeddings.
help.metaBtnDesc: Generates "metadata chunks" for all active files including DWG/MAX. Brings AI chat search coverage closer to sidebar search.
help.purgeBtnDesc: Removes obsolete chunk records from old versions. Does not touch files.
help.citationChipDesc: Click → the related source card flashes and scrolls into view. Also opens the asset detail panel.
help.sourceCardDesc: File name + page + short preview + score. Click → open asset detail.
help.stopBtnDesc: You can stop mid-answer. The partial answer is saved.
help.newSessionDesc: Open a blank chat. After the first Q&A, the LLM auto-generates a title.
help.deleteSessionDesc: Deletes the chat and all its messages. Undo with Ctrl+Z.
help.antiHallucinationDesc: If none of the query keywords appear in any indexed file, the LLM is not called and "no info found" is returned.
help.conversationCtxDesc: The last 4 messages are added to the prompt as PREVIOUS CONVERSATION. Follow-up questions ("who is that?", "which project?") work.
help.autoIndexDesc: When a new folder is scanned, all new files including DWG are indexed as metadata chunks in the background, ready for chat.

synthesis.pickTitle: Pick documents for synthesis — per-document section + comparison

assetPicker.title: Pick documents for synthesis
assetPicker.subtitle: Each selected document gets its own section + a comparison summary.
assetPicker.searchPlaceholder: Search by file name or path…
assetPicker.empty: No indexed documents yet. First index files via Chat → 📊 Index button.

dwgWarning.title: Visual similarity is unreliable for technical drawings
dwgWarning.body: CLIP-based visual search produces weak signal on DWG/DXF drawings. For more accurate results, search by layer/block name text or use a geometric shape query (e.g. "octagonal pool", "5x10 m rectangular rooms").

booleanSearch.hint: Tip: Use AND, OR, NOT operators and "quoted phrases"
booleanSearch.examples: Example: plan AND kitchen · facade OR section · hall NOT parking · "floor plan"

shapeSearch.title: Geometric Shape Search
shapeSearch.dropHint: Drag & drop an image or click to select (PNG, JPG, BMP)
shapeSearch.dropSubHint: The geometric shape in the image will be extracted and matched against DWG files

step0.desc: Ollama is required for AI features (chat, visual search, tag suggestions). Ollama is a free tool that runs AI models locally on your computer.
step0.installGuide: If Ollama is not installed, download it from ollama.com/download, install it, and return to this step.

step1.desc: Two models are needed for AI: one for text questions and one for image analysis. You can download missing models with a single click.
step1.chatMissingModel: {{model}} missing — run in terminal: ollama pull {{model}}
step1.altHint: Or you can select one of the installed models from Advanced Model Settings: {{models}}
step1.visionMissingModel: {{model}} missing — run in terminal: ollama pull {{model}}
step1.hint: Text model ({{chatModel}}, ~2.5 GB) and vision model ({{visionModel}}, ~4.7 GB) will be downloaded. This may take a few minutes depending on your internet connection.

step2.title: AI Ready!
step2.desc: All AI features are ready to use. You can ask questions about your archive, analyze images, and use smart search.

tagManager.confirmMerge: "{{source}}" tag will be merged into "{{target}}". Source tag will be deleted. Continue?

xmp.exportTitle: Export this file's metadata as an XMP sidecar file
xmp.fallbackNote: ({{count}} files write-protected — saved to backup location)
xmp.fallbackSingle: (Folder is write-protected — saved to backup location)

help.title: What is XMP Sidecar Export?
help.body: XMP (Extensible Metadata Platform) is an international metadata standard developed by Adobe. A .xmp file placed alongside each file carries tags, project name, approval status, version, and other information. Even if the file is moved or the database is deleted, the metadata is preserved. Adobe Bridge, Lightroom, and other DAM tools can read this format.
help.howTo: Usage: Click the 'XMP' button in the file detail panel, or use 'Create XMP Sidecar for All' from the source folder menu. If the folder is write-protected, files are automatically saved to the application data folder.

ragSensitivity.title: AI Sensitivity Filter
ragSensitivity.subtitle: Exclude sensitive data from AI chat
ragSensitivity.enabled: Filter active — sensitive data hidden from AI
ragSensitivity.disabled: Filter off — AI can access all data
